page: Microsoft Azure D4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the D-family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The configuration includes 8 dedicated physical cores with 8 vCPUs, providing 1.0 thread per core. Memory capacity is set at 28.0 GB, which equates to 3.5 GB of RAM per core. The instance is bundled with 410 GB of local storage, but does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the bottom 25% tier. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also show weak performance. Conversely, multi-core and memory benchmarks consistently rank in the middle 50% tier. This average performance is demonstrated by a stress-ng multi-core score of 8196.1, a Geekbench multi-core score of 5821.0, and a Passmark CPU score of 11118.32. Memory bandwidth is stable, with cached small-block reads reaching 720.66 GB/sec and uncached large-block writes at 36.46 GB/sec.

From a resource density perspective, the D4 offers a balanced allocation of CPU and memory, supplemented by local storage. It is suitable for workloads that can leverage multi-threaded execution to offset the weaker single-core performance. Factual benchmark results indicate average capabilities in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput. Consequently, this server is positioned for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and moderate database operations.
description: Microsoft Azure D4 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 8 dedicated physical cores, 28.0 GB of system memory, and 410 GB of local storage, with no integrated GPU. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance exhibits a performance profile characterized by weak single-core metrics but average multi-core and memory capabilities. Specifically, single-core Geekbench and stress-ng tests rank in the bottom 25%, while multi-core benchmarks, memory bandwidth, and database operations consistently fall within the middle 50% tier. With a balanced resource density, the D4 is suitable for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations that do not require high single-threaded performance.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring an 8-core Intel Xeon 8272CL processor, 28.0 GB of RAM, and 410 GB of local storage, delivering average multi-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4 is an 8-vCPU virtual machine with an Intel Xeon processor, 28.0 GB RAM, and 410 GB local storage for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An 8-core x86_64 virtual machine with balanced memory and local storage, optimized for multi-threaded general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 8 dedicated physical cores at 2.6 GHz
28.0 GB of system memory and 410 GB of bundled local storage
Average multi-core performance and memory bandwidth in benchmark testing
Weaker single-core and single-threaded execution speeds
Suited for static web serving, software compilation, and database operations
